cards.title: Requential Coding Achieves SOTA Generalization Bounds for Billion-Parameter LLMs
cards.why_it_matters: As an AI/ML engineer tracking scaling theory and generalization, this directly addresses why large models generalize despite overparameterization and yields the tightest known PAC-Bayes bounds for LLMs.
cards.summary: Requential coding compresses models by having a teacher sample from the student's own distribution; the student's code records only disagreements, making code length independent of parameter count and data entropy. Applied to billion-parameter LLMs, it yields state-of-the-art PAC-Bayes generalization bounds that tighten with scale in the compute-optimal regime, outperforming aggressive quantization-based bounds. It also detects overfitting across epochs and separates learnable from random content in datasets.

cards.title: First Comprehensive Survey of Metacognition in LLMs: Methods, Benchmarks, and Open Problems
cards.why_it_matters: For an AI/research engineer building reliable LLM-based systems, understanding the current state of metacognitive capabilities—calibration, self-monitoring, uncertainty—directly informs design and evaluation choices.
cards.summary: This survey taxonomizes metacognition research in LLMs, covering methods to measure, elicit, and improve metacognitive abilities alongside relevant benchmarks. It reviews findings on when and how LLMs exhibit self-monitoring and uncertainty awareness, discusses applications to reliability and transparency, and identifies open challenges. Positioned as the first comprehensive overview of this emerging subfield.

cards.title: Low-Dimensional Invariant Manifold Theory Explains Inductive Reasoning in Transformers
cards.why_it_matters: This theoretical result is directly relevant to mechanistic interpretability and circuit analysis work, offering a principled, low-dimensional framework to predict how attention heads form circuits during training.
cards.summary: The paper proves that transformer training dynamics for a generalized class of inductive tasks—unifying in-context n-grams and multi-hop reasoning—are confined to a low-dimensional invariant manifold. A handful of interpretable coordinates capture learning dynamics rather than millions of parameters. The framework characterizes in-context vs. in-weights learning competition, how initialization selects circuits, and enables automatic circuit detection in trained models.

cards.title: REGRIND: Single-Demo Retargeting-Guided RL Achieves Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation
cards.why_it_matters: Robotics and RL researchers will find this directly relevant as it extends the retargeting-plus-RL recipe proven in humanoid locomotion to contact-rich dexterous manipulation with zero-shot hardware transfer.
cards.summary: REGRIND retargets a single human hand-object demonstration to a robot reference preserving hand-object spatial and contact relationships, trains a residual RL policy to track object-centric keypoints in simulation, then transfers zero-shot to hardware via system identification. Demonstrated on two multi-fingered hands for scissors operation and screwdriver turning. The paper systematically analyzes key factors governing sim-to-real transfer in contact-rich settings.

cards.title: Pruned Shadow Hamiltonian Simulation Cuts Qubit Register Size for Real-Time Dynamics
cards.why_it_matters: Quantum ML researchers and those tracking quantum computing infrastructure will want to know about this efficiency advance in Hamiltonian simulation, which directly reduces qubit resource requirements.
cards.summary: The algorithm approximates real-time observable dynamics under time-independent Hamiltonians by working in the operator algebra generated by commutators, then pruning it to only the elements most relevant to target observables. Two pruning schemes are proposed—predefined operator basis and Krylov basis—plus a hybrid. Benchmarked on 1D and 2D lattice spin systems for one- and higher-point correlators.
